stocks.news: Rivian's stock pops as the EV maker becomes the latest to partner with Uber. Uber plans to invest as much as $1.25 billion in the EV maker as they work together on robotaxis.

economy.commentary:
The Conference Board Leading Economic Index (LEI) for the US fell slightly in January, signaling continued caution ahead.

Gold pulled back to $4,662 after recent highs, while silver surged to $72.08 on strong industrial demand. Brent crude held steady at $107.57. The 10-year Treasury yield dipped to 4.25% as investors digest the Fed's decision to hold rates steady amid lingering inflation concerns and geopolitical uncertainty.

crypto.commentary:
Crypto.com cuts 12% of staff as it integrates AI across the business for efficiency. The exchange laid off about 180 employees as it restructures and rolls out enterprise-wide AI to drive efficiencies.

Bitcoin rebounded to $70,082 while Ethereum held at $2,129 and XRP remained flat at $1.44 as markets stabilize following the Fed's rate decision.

real_estate.homebuilder: Japanese homebuilders are on a massive U.S. buying spree, including Sumitomo Forestry's $4.5 billion plan to acquire Tri-Pointe Homes, as they look to capitalize on the 4.03 million home shortage in America.
real_estate.commentary:
The typical home that went under contract in February spent 66 days on the market — the slowest February pace in a decade. The typical buyer scored 1.8% off the list price — the biggest February discount since 2023; sellers outnumber buyers, giving buyers negotiating power.

Pending home sales and new listings both inched down last month, while home prices inched up. The Bay Area market is "quietly accelerating" into the spring season; while overall inventory remains tight, single-family homes in Alameda County have climbed back to a median of $1.3 million, showing resilience despite higher rates.

📍 Latest Real Estate News in the Bay Area: The spring market is showing a distinct "selective rebound." In Alameda County (Fremont), the median single-family home price reached $1.3 million in February, essentially flat year-over-year, while the condo market dipped significantly to a median of $550,000. Santa Clara County remains one of the most resilient submarkets, where well-priced single-family homes are moving in an average of just 14 days. However, buyers are becoming increasingly price-sensitive; luxury inventory now requires much sharper positioning to attract serious offers compared to entry-level homes.

📍 Latest General News in the Bay Area: A Heat Advisory is in effect across Santa Clara County (Milpitas/Cupertino) through today, March 20, as unseasonably hot weather moves through the region. In local government, Santa Clara County recently received renewed grant funding to provide free child safety seats and education to low-income families. In Fremont, the community continues its "platinum" 70th-anniversary celebrations, with the Washington Township Museum of Local History featuring a new exhibit on the five original towns that merged to form the city. Meanwhile, on the state level, Assembly Speaker Robert Rivas has officially honored MariaElena De La Garza as the 2026 Woman of the Year for the region.
